free_text: Elastic scattering of 120, 145 and 172.5 MeV
alpha-particles by 12C, 24Mg and 27Al
and optical model analysis
Similar to J,APP/B,12,491,1981
SD
On. Major revisions in 002-004, 006.
Self supporting natural C foils (~3 mg/cm2 thick)
The zero point of the absolute angular scale was
determined to be better than 0.03 deg.
The absolute cross section error (common normalization
error) is estimated to be less than 15%.
The relative error is smaller than 10%.
The main uncertainties in the evaluation of the cross
sections were those due to  target thickness, beam
current integration and solid angles.
No information on the source of uncertainty
Printout sent to A.Ingemarsson from S.Wiktor
On. Numerical data expression revised.
Self supporting highly enriched 24Mg (~3 mg/cm2 thick)
The zero point of the absolute angular scale was
determined to be better than 0.03 deg.

The relative error is smaller than 10%.
The main uncertainties in the evaluation of the cross
sections were those due to  target thickness, beam
current integration and solid angles.
No information on the source of uncertainty
Printout sent to A.Ingemarsson from S.Wiktor
On. Data corrected at 23.17 deg.
Self supporting highly enriched 24Mg (~3 mg/cm2 thick)
Fig.1 of Acta Phys.Pol.B12(1981)491
On. Numerical data expression revised.
